entries.definition:
f–k–k to dissemble, to dismantle, to separate, to free from
bondage; to be weak; jaw. Of this root, two forms occur once
each in the Qur’an: فَكّ fakk and مُنْفَكِّين munfakkīn.

فَكّ fakk [v. n.] freeing, setting free, untying *(90:13) فَكُّ رَقَبَةٍ
freeing of a slave [lit. untying of a neck].

مُنْفَكّ munfakk [act./pass. part.] one who ceases an action, one
who desists, one who leaves off, one who lets go of; something
untied (98:1) لَمْ يَكُنِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ مُنْفَكِّينَ حَتَّى تَأْتِيَهُمُ
الْبَيِّنَةُ those who denied [the truth] of the People of the Book and
the idolaters would never desist till the Clear Sign came to them.
